skill.name: using-email-templates
skill.description: Guides users through Mailtrap-hosted email templates, Handlebars variables, template UUID payloads, and sandbox testing for transactional or bulk email sends.
skill.summary: Create, test, and send Mailtrap-hosted email templates with Handlebars personalization.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution and weak cryptography, but those are false positives from Markdown backticks and YAML description text in SKILL.md. The confirmed risk is legitimate network email-sending guidance that uses bearer tokens and live Mailtrap endpoints, so publication is acceptable with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Email Sending Workflow With Bearer Token
medium_findings.description: The skill provides Mailtrap send endpoint guidance and a curl example using Authorization: Bearer $MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN. This is a legitimate Mailtrap workflow, but generated commands could send email or expose tokens if copied into unsafe logs or shared environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network send path and bearer token usage are explicit in the documented example. The intent is legitimate product usage, so the concern is operational misuse rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated inline Markdown code and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md is documentation, and no executable Ruby code or dynamic command execution was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows Markdown formatting around field names, headers, and examples rather than executable source code. There is no command interpolation or runtime path in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern In Description Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on YAML front matter lines, but those lines contain the skill description. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing routine, or encryption call is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain English metadata about Mailtrap templates. There is no code construct that could invoke DES, MD5, SHA1, or another weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: External Documentation And SDK Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to Mailtrap documentation and SDK repositories. These links are expected for an integration guide, but users should rely on official sources and avoid pasting credentials into third-party pages.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced domains are Mailtrap documentation and Mailtrap GitHub SDK repositories. This is consistent with the skill purpose and does not show exfiltration or malicious redirection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request Example To Email Send API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a curl POST example for the Mailtrap send API. This pattern can trigger outbound email if used with a valid token and recipient, so users should prefer sandbox testing for risky changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The example is a direct HTTP POST to the Mailtrap send endpoint with a bearer token header. It is documented and legitimate, but it has real side effects when executed.

content.value_statement: Email template integration often fails because payload fields, variables, and send streams are easy to mix up. This skill gives focused Mailtrap guidance for template UUIDs, Handlebars data, sandbox previews, and SDK references.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Mailtrap-hosted templates for transactional and bulk sends.
Shows the payload fields for template UUIDs and Handlebars variables.
Points users to official Mailtrap API, sandbox, and SDK documentation.
Clarifies escaped, raw, conditional, loop, and nested Handlebars patterns.
Encourages sandbox previews before changing risky email templates.
limitations: It does not manage templates directly inside the Mailtrap UI.
It does not validate live API credentials or account permissions.
It does not guarantee current SDK method signatures without checking linked repositories.
It does not replace email-client rendering tests for production templates.

use_cases.title: Add a transactional template send
use_cases.description: Build an API payload that references a Mailtrap template UUID and passes the correct personalization variables.

use_cases.title: Prepare a campaign template
use_cases.description: Separate promotional email design from application code while keeping bulk send payloads aligned with Mailtrap requirements.

use_cases.title: Debug missing template data
use_cases.description: Compare Handlebars variables with the request body and test the result through sandbox preview flows.

prompt_templates.title: Find the right payload fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me send a Mailtrap-hosted template. I have a template UUID and need the required payload fields for a transactional email.

prompt_templates.title: Map variables to Handlebars
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Handlebars variables and tell me what template_variables object I should send with Mailtrap.

prompt_templates.title: Create responsive template HTML
prompt_templates.prompt: Create mobile-compatible HTML for a Mailtrap email template using these brand details, sections, and personalization variables.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot a template send
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this Mailtrap template send issue. Check stream choice, template_uuid usage, Handlebars variables, sandbox testing, and SDK documentation gaps.

output_examples.input: I need to send a welcome template with a user name and plan name.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the template UUID field, lists the needed variable names, and recommends a sandbox preview before live delivery.

output_examples.input: My template shows blank order details.
output_examples.output: The response compares nested Handlebars paths with the supplied variable object and highlights missing or mismatched keys.

output_examples.input: I want one template for bulk and transactional messages.
output_examples.output: The response explains stream selection, endpoint differences, and why each send flow should be tested separately.

best_practices: Check the official Mailtrap docs before generating request bodies or SDK calls.
Use sandbox previews for risky template changes before sending live email.
Keep API tokens in environment variables and avoid sharing command logs that include headers.
anti_patterns: Do not mix raw text or HTML body fields with template UUID fields unless the API schema allows it.
Do not assume Handlebars variable names match application field names without checking the template.
Do not send live bulk or transactional email before verifying rendering and recipient data.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you create, test, and send Mailtrap-hosted email templates with template UUIDs and Handlebars variables.

faq.question: Can it create template HTML?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide generation of responsive email HTML that is suitable for Mailtrap templates.

faq.question: Does it send emails by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. A user or application must run the final Mailtrap request.

faq.question: Which Mailtrap streams does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers transactional and bulk template sends, with reminders to choose the correct stream and endpoint.

faq.question: How should I test a template safely?
faq.answer: Use Mailtrap sandbox previews and test sends before changing production templates or live recipients.

faq.question: Does it replace official SDK documentation?
faq.answer: No. It instructs users to check current SDK repositories for exact method signatures.
